title: Detection and Flagging of Suspicious Withdrawal Patterns
description: The system shall detect suspicious withdrawal patterns, such as rapid successive withdrawals or unusual amounts, and flag these patterns for review.
rationale: To ensure the security of cash disbursements by promptly identifying potential fraudulent activities that could compromise the integrity of the settlement engine.
business_value: This requirement enhances the reliability and security of the settlement engine, reducing the risk of unauthorized transactions and protecting financial assets.

source.description: As a system I need to detect suspicious withdrawal patterns (e.g., rapid successive withdrawals, unusual amounts) So that they can be flagged for review

acceptance_criteria: When a withdrawal pattern involves rapid successive withdrawals or unusual amounts, then the pattern is flagged for review.
postconditions: The suspicious withdrawal pattern has been identified and flagged for review.
functional_constraints: Detection must be based on criteria like rapid successive withdrawals or unusual amounts.
Flagged patterns will be subject to manual or automated review.
security_requirements: The system shall detect suspicious withdrawal patterns without false positives or negatives.
Pattern detection and flagging mechanisms must operate within a secure environment.

non_functional_requirements.performance: Detection of suspicious patterns should be completed within 100 milliseconds for real-time processing.
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system must be available 99.9% of the time to ensure continuous monitoring.
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system shall handle up to 1,000 withdrawal requests per second without performance degradation.
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error handling should provide clear and actionable feedback for flagged patterns.

edge_cases: Concurrent rapid successive withdrawals from multiple users
Withdrawal patterns involving unusual amounts that are not fraudulent (e.g., large transactions by legitimate users)
System under heavy load during withdrawal processing

interfaces.name: Withdrawal API
interfaces.description: Interface through which withdrawal requests are submitted to the system.

interfaces.name: Transaction Database
interfaces.description: Stores historical transaction data for pattern detection.

test_cases.description: Multiple rapid successive withdrawals from a single user
test_cases.expected_result: Pattern flagged and submitted for review

test_cases.description: Large transaction by a legitimate user
test_cases.expected_result: No pattern flagged, normal processing continues
